title: ImportError: No module named 'requests'
description: pytest failed with ImportError: No module named 'requests' in test_api.py at line 3. CI pipeline broke after a fresh environment was spun up.
resolution: Added 'requests>=2.31.0' to requirements.txt and re-ran pip install -r requirements.txt in the workflow.

title: AssertionError in test_user_creation — expected 201 got 500
description: test_auth.py::test_user_creation failed with AssertionError: assert 500 == 201. The database was not seeded before the test ran, causing a foreign key constraint violation.
resolution: Added a pytest fixture to create required database tables before each test class using conftest.py and @pytest.fixture(autouse=True).

title: ConnectionRefusedError: PostgreSQL not available in CI
description: Multiple tests failed with ConnectionRefusedError: [Errno 111] Connection refused when attempting to connect to localhost:5432. PostgreSQL service was not started in the GitHub Actions workflow.
resolution: Added a 'services: postgres:' block to the GitHub Actions workflow YAML and waited for it to become healthy using a healthcheck step.

title: Jest: Cannot find module '@/components/Button'
description: Jest test suite failed with Cannot find module '@/components/Button' from 'src/pages/Home.test.js'. Path alias was not configured in jest.config.js.
resolution: Added moduleNameMapper to jest.config.js to resolve the '@/' alias: { '^@/(.*)$': '<rootDir>/src/$1' }

title: Python TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable
description: test_search.py::test_keyword_search raised TypeError: argument of type 'NoneType' is not iterable. The function returned None when the database was empty instead of returning an empty list.
resolution: Fixed the function to return [] instead of None when no results are found. Added a None guard and updated the test to cover the empty-state case.

title: GitHub Actions: Process completed with exit code 1 — pip install failed
description: CI workflow step 'Install dependencies' failed with exit code 1. pip could not resolve conflicting package versions between Flask 3.0 and an older extension requiring Flask 2.x.
resolution: Pinned Flask to 2.3.3 and upgraded the extension to a version compatible with Flask 3. Used pip-compile to generate a reproducible lock file.

title: SyntaxError: invalid syntax in config.py
description: pytest collection failed with SyntaxError: invalid syntax at config.py:14. A walrus operator (:=) was used in Python 3.7 which does not support it.
resolution: Changed the Python version in the GitHub Actions workflow from 3.7 to 3.8+, or rewrote the expression without the walrus operator for backwards compatibility.

title: Jest timeout — async test exceeded 5000ms
description: FAIL src/api/users.test.js — Test exceeded timeout of 5000ms. An API call inside the test hit a real endpoint instead of the mocked one because jest.mock() was called after the import.
resolution: Moved jest.mock() calls to the top of the test file, before any imports that use the module. Verified the mock intercepted the fetch call by checking jest.fn() call counts.

title: KeyError: 'DATABASE_URL' — missing environment variable
description: test_db.py::test_connection raised KeyError: 'DATABASE_URL'. The environment variable was defined in .env but not exported to the GitHub Actions runner environment.
resolution: Added DATABASE_URL as a repository secret in GitHub (Settings > Secrets) and referenced it in the workflow with ${{ secrets.DATABASE_URL }}.

title: npm ERR! peer dependency conflict — React 18 vs React 17
description: npm install failed with ERESOLVE unable to resolve dependency tree. A UI component library required React 17 while the project had upgraded to React 18.
resolution: Upgraded the component library to a version that supports React 18, or used --legacy-peer-deps as a temporary workaround while tracking the upstream issue.

title: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'json'
description: test_client.py::test_post_endpoint raised AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'json'. The requests.post() call returned None because the test used a real HTTP client without starting the test server.
resolution: Switched to Flask's built-in test client: client = app.test_client(). No running server needed; the test client handles requests in-process.

title: PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied — writing to /etc
description: CI pipeline step failed with PermissionError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: '/etc/app/config.json'. The application tried to write its config to a system directory at startup.
resolution: Changed the config file path to use the user's home directory (~/.config/app/) or a temporary directory (/tmp/). Added XDG_CONFIG_HOME environment variable support.

title: Docker build failed — COPY file not found
description: GitHub Actions 'Build Docker image' step failed: COPY failed: file not found in build context: requirements.txt. The Docker build context was set to a subdirectory that didn't contain requirements.txt.
resolution: Changed the docker build command to run from the repository root: docker build -f docker/Dockerfile . instead of docker build docker/. The build context needs to include all COPY source paths.

title: RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded
description: test_serializer.py::test_circular_reference raised RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded. A circular model relationship caused the JSON serializer to recurse infinitely.
resolution: Added a seen_ids set to the serializer to track already-visited objects and break the cycle. Alternatively, used SerializerMethodField to manually control nested depth.

title: GitHub Actions: actions/checkout failed — token expired
description: Workflow failed at actions/checkout@v3 with error: remote: Repository not found or token expired. The repository's GITHUB_TOKEN did not have sufficient permissions for a private dependency.
resolution: Generated a Personal Access Token (PAT) with repo scope, stored it as a secret (GH_PAT), and configured git to use it: git config url.'https://TOKEN@github.com/'.insteadOf 'https://github.com/'
